steps.title: Double the surface area
steps.content: The patio has area $d$. Covering it twice means the stain must cover $2d$ square feet.

steps.title: Convert area to gallons
steps.content:
Each gallon covers $240$ square feet, so the number of gallons needed is
$$\frac{2d}{240}=\frac{d}{120}$$

steps.title: Multiply by the price
steps.content:
Each gallon costs $27$ dollars, so the cost is
$$c=27(\frac{d}{120})$$The answer is C.
